PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION SUBMITTED IN OPPOSITION IS AMBIGUOUS. PLAINTIFF DESCRIBES "REMOVING BUFFALO PUMPS, INSTALLED NEW ONES AND REBUILT THEM." PLAINTIFF THEN GOES ON TO DESCRIBE HIS WORK ON "INTERNAL GASKETS ON BUFFALO PUMPS" BUT DOES NOT STATE WHETHER THE PUMPS WERE NEW AND/OR REBUILT. THE QUESTION REMAINS UNANSWERED. This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ROSENDAHL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Nevin of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Keri Avellini of Jackson & Wallace (982.6300) for defendant and moving party

This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted based on plaintiff's responses to special interrogatories 4b, 5, 6b, 7, 19 and 12 and 13. Defendant has demonstrated that plaintiff lacks evidence to support the contention that the valves plaintiff worked on contained asbestos manufactured or sold by defendant. Plaintiff's declaration submitted in opposition is ambiguous. Plaintiff describes "removing Buffalo Pumps, installed new ones and rebuilt them." Plaintiff then goes on to describe his work on "internal gaskets on Buffalo Pumps" but does not state whether the pumps were new and/or rebuilt. The question remains unanswered. What evidence does plaintiff have that the pumps he worked on contained asbestos for which Buffalo Pumps is responsible? Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
